items.title: Factoring in the reported contracts for John Collins, Kevin Huerter & Javonte Green and the Gary Harris/Taurean Prince acquisitions, I've got the Pistons at about $42.8M under the luxury tax.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Factoring in the reported contracts for John Collins, Kevin Huerter & Javonte Green and the Gary Harris/Taurean Prince acquisitions, I've got the Pistons at about $42.8M under the luxury tax.

That's enough room to give Jalen Duren a 25% of the cap max without going into the tax.

items.title: Detroit Pistons salary cap situation after signing John Collins and Kevin Huerter.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Detroit Pistons salary cap situation after signing John Collins and Kevin Huerter.

They have $40 million in luxury tax space to re-sign Jalen Duren, but could create more room.

Collins is getting more than the mid-level exception, which means cap space or sign and trade.

media.title: Kevin Huerter re-signing with Detroit Pistons for 3 years, $27 million
media.description: The Detroit Pistons re-signed Kevin Huerter to a three-year, $27 million contract, a league source confirmed to the Free Press, on Monday, June 29.

items.title: Mentioned this on the pod (and I'm spitballing here), but let's say the Pistons re-sign Harris and Huerter, use the MLE and go into next trade deadline with more tradable salary than they currently have. Could allow them to make a move without trading Stew/Holland/Robinson. Is th
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Mentioned this on the pod (and I'm spitballing here), but let's say the Pistons re-sign Harris and Huerter, use the MLE and go into next trade deadline with more tradable salary than they currently have. Could allow them to make a move without trading Stew/Holland/Robinson. Is that bad?
